title: Quantization Error Propagation: Revisiting Layer-Wise Post-Training Quantization

repos.name: FujitsuResearch__qep
repos.evidence: README cites exactly this paper ('Quantization Error Propagation: Revisiting Layer-Wise Post-Training Quantization', Yamato Arai and Yuma Ichikawa, NeurIPS 2025) and matches the paper's stated code URL (https://github.com/FujitsuResearch/qep). Repo owner FujitsuResearch matches both authors' affiliation (Fujitsu Limited). Core source files qep_awq.py, gptq.py, llama.py implement the QEP weight correction W += (W @ H_delta @ Hinv) * perccorr matching paper Eq. (6).

rationale: Exactly one repo was cloned (FujitsuResearch/qep). Its README explicitly cites this paper by full title, both author names, and NeurIPS 2025 venue; the paper itself lists this GitHub URL as the official code link. The repo implements the paper's named method QEP (Quantization Error Propagation) with the closed-form weight correction that matches paper Eq. (6), along with RTN/GPTQ/AWQ/QuIP drivers. All audit findings are written against this core repo and address genuine reproducibility gaps (missing scipy/portalocker in requirements, missing Fig. 2 error-accumulation script, missing Table 3 timing harness, missing OmniQuant runner, incomplete README, per-layer alpha not exposed in CLI, best-of-5-seed reporting), none of which misattribute findings to a wrong repository.
audit_impact: None. The audit correctly identified the core repo and wrote substantive findings about real reproducibility issues. No misclassification occurred.
recommended_action: No action required. The provenance is correct.
